meta.note: India targeted top-up. The DB already holds ~124 India titles (the full Wikipedia 'List of books banned in India', the Kashmir cluster incl. the 2025 J&K notification titles, and most colonial-era customs bans). This batch adds title-specific bans NOT already present: recent publisher withdrawals / court restraints (2014–2022), three Punjab 2022 textbook bans, a UP and a Karnataka state action, and two well-sourced older proscriptions. The two 'History of Punjab' textbooks share a title so are disambiguated by author to avoid a slug collision. Defamation-driven restraints use reason 'other'.

entries.title: Delhi Riots 2020: The Untold Story
entries.description_ban: Bloomsbury India withdrew the book pre-publication on 22 August 2020 after backlash over a launch event; later republished by Garuda Prakashan.
entries.inclusion_rationale: High-profile publisher withdrawal under pressure; reported by The News Minute.

entries.title: Godman to Tycoon: The Untold Story of Baba Ramdev
entries.description_ban: A Delhi district court issued an ex parte injunction (Aug 2017) restraining Juggernaut from publishing/selling the biography; the Delhi High Court reaffirmed the restraint in Sep 2018 unless passages were cut.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered restraint on a specific title; reported by Scroll.in.

entries.title: Communalism and Sexual Violence: Ahmedabad Since 1969
entries.description_ban: Orient BlackSwan withdrew the book weeks after release, citing fear of reprisals, after a legal notice from the RSS-linked Shiksha Bachao Andolan Samiti; reissued by Tulika Books in 2017.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Publisher withdrawal under legal pressure; reported by The Caravan.

entries.title: Gunning for the Godman: The True Story Behind Asaram Bapu's Conviction
entries.description_ban: A New Delhi district court stayed publication in September 2020 on a co-accused's plea citing reputational harm; the stay was later vacated.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered publication stay on a specific title; reported by The Week.

entries.title: Modern ABC of History of Punjab
entries.description_ban: Banned by the Punjab School Education Board (May 2022) for 'distorting' Sikh history and Gurbani; the Punjab state government ordered action against the author and publisher.
entries.inclusion_rationale: State (Punjab) textbook ban; reported by Scroll.in.

entries.title: History of Punjab (Class 12, Mahinderpal Kaur)
entries.description_ban: One of three Class-12 history textbooks banned by the Punjab School Education Board (May 2022) for distorting Sikh history.
entries.inclusion_rationale: State (Punjab) textbook ban; reported by India TV.

entries.title: History of Punjab (Class 12, M.S. Mann)
entries.description_ban: Third Class-12 history textbook banned in the same Punjab School Education Board notification (May 2022) for distorting Sikh history.
entries.inclusion_rationale: State (Punjab) textbook ban; reported by ETV Bharat.

entries.title: Rani
entries.description_ban: Banned by the Uttar Pradesh government (Mayawati administration) in February 2008 over a fictionalised romance between Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi and a British officer deemed objectionable.
entries.inclusion_rationale: State (Uttar Pradesh) ban; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: Tipu Nija Kanasugalu
entries.description_ban: A Bengaluru court (Karnataka) stayed the sale and distribution of this Kannada book ('Real Dreams of Tipu') in November 2022 after a Wakf Board complaint that it used a slur and distorted Tipu Sultan's history to hurt Muslim sentiment.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered restraint (Karnataka); reported by The Wire.

entries.title: British Rule in India
entries.description_ban: Nationalist-sympathetic indictment of British rule, reprinted by the Ghadar movement's Yugantar Ashram; the British blocked its import under the Sea Customs Act and it survives in the British Library proscribed-publications collection.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Raj-era customs proscription of a specific title; reported by Scroll.in (British Library collection).

entries.title: Testament of Subhas Bose
entries.description_ban: Posthumous compilation of Bose's broadcasts and statements criticising British rule; banned and copies confiscated from the publisher in July 1946, then un-banned within two months after Sardar Patel's intervention.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific late-colonial ban; documented in Devika Sethi's 'Banned & Censored' (reviewed in The Tribune).
